items.question: How do I qualify for credit card bonuses?
items.answer: Most signup bonuses require you to spend a certain amount within the first 3-6 months. Check the specific offer terms for exact requirements.

items.question: What's a good APY for savings?
items.answer: High-yield savings accounts currently offer between 4-5% APY. Compare rates across multiple banks to find the best deal.

items.question: Will applying for multiple cards hurt my credit?
items.answer: Each application creates a hard inquiry, which may temporarily lower your score by a few points. Space out applications every 6 months.

items.question: Are online banks safe?
items.answer: Yes, reputable online banks are FDIC or NCUA insured, protecting your deposits up to $250,000.

items.question: How do I choose between rewards and low APR?
items.answer: If you carry a balance monthly, prioritize low APR. If you pay in full, rewards cards offer better value.

items.question: Can I negotiate loan rates?
items.answer: Yes! Many lenders will negotiate rates, especially if you have good credit or existing relationship with the bank.
